title: Reverse Nodes in k-Group
description:
Given the `head` of a linked list, reverse the nodes `k` at a time and return
the modified list. Nodes left over at the end (fewer than `k`) stay in their
original order. Lists are given/returned as arrays.

starterCode.python:
# Definition for singly-linked list node: node.val, node.next
def reverse_k_group(head, k):
    # Reverse the list k nodes at a time.
    pass

solutions.summary: Reverse the first k nodes, then recurse on the rest and link them.

steps.title: Check there are k nodes, then reverse them
steps.explanation: Walk k nodes ahead; if fewer remain, leave them. Otherwise reverse this group and connect it to the recursively processed remainder.

code.python:
# Definition for singly-linked list node: node.val, node.next
def reverse_k_group(head, k):
    node = head
    count = 0
    while node and count < k:
        node = node.next
        count += 1
    if count < k:
        return head
    prev = reverse_k_group(node, k)
    cur = head
    for _ in range(k):
        nxt = cur.next
        cur.next = prev
        prev = cur
        cur = nxt
    return prev
